skill.name: azure-messaging-webpubsub-java
skill.description: Build real-time web applications with Azure Web PubSub SDK for Java. Use when implementing WebSocket-based messaging, live updates, chat applications, or server-to-client push notifications.
skill.summary: Real-time messaging SDK for Java with WebSocket support and group management
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation containing Java code examples in markdown format. Detected backticks are markdown formatting, not shell execution. URLs are placeholder examples for environment configuration. No cryptographic algorithms are used. Skill is safe for publication.

content.user_title: Build real-time apps with Azure Web PubSub
content.value_statement: Enable real-time bidirectional communication in your Java applications. Azure Web PubSub SDK provides WebSocket-based messaging with groups, user targeting, and access control.
actual_capabilities: Create synchronous and asynchronous Web PubSub clients
Send messages to all connections, groups, specific users, or individual connections
Manage group memberships and connection lifecycle
Generate client access tokens with custom roles and expiration
Filter messages using OData-like query expressions
Grant and revoke permissions for group operations
limitations: Requires Azure Web PubSub service subscription and configuration
Connection management relies on client-side implementation
Message size limits enforced by Azure service quotas
Does not handle client-side WebSocket connection logic

use_cases.title: Chat Application Backend
use_cases.description: Implement multi-room chat with user targeting, group messaging, and presence tracking using hub and group abstractions.

use_cases.title: Live Dashboard Updates
use_cases.description: Push real-time notifications, metrics, or data updates to connected browser clients without polling.
use_cases.target_user: Backend engineers creating monitoring or analytics dashboards

use_cases.title: Multiplayer Game Server
use_cases.description: Coordinate game state updates between players using targeted group messaging and connection management.
use_cases.target_user: Game developers implementing browser-based multiplayer experiences

prompt_templates.title: Basic Client Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Azure Web PubSub client in Java using a connection string. Initialize it for a hub named 'notifications' and send a test message to all connections.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with Azure Web PubSub SDK

prompt_templates.title: Group-Based Messaging
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement a messaging system where users can be added to groups like 'premium-users' and 'announcements'. Send different messages to each group and manage group memberships dynamically.

prompt_templates.title: Token Generation with Roles
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate client access tokens for users joining a chat hub. Assign roles for joining groups and sending messages. Set token expiration to 2 hours and auto-join the 'lobby' group.
prompt_templates.scenario: Securing client connections with fine-grained permissions

prompt_templates.title: Async Operations with Error Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the async Web PubSub client to broadcast messages to all connections. Implement reactive error handling that logs failures and retries transient errors. Handle HttpResponseException for invalid connection states.

output_examples.input: Initialize client and send welcome message
output_examples.output: Client connected to hub 'chat'. Message 'Hello everyone!' sent to 47 connections via sendToAll.

output_examples.input: Add user to group and send targeted notification
output_examples.output: User 'user123' added to group 'premium-users'. Notification sent to 12 connections in group. Token expires in 3600 seconds.

best_practices: Organize connections into groups for efficient targeted messaging and reduced bandwidth
Set appropriate token expiration times and grant minimal required permissions via roles
Use separate hubs to isolate different application features and scale independently
anti_patterns: Do not embed connection strings or access keys directly in source code
Avoid sending large payloads through Web PubSub - use it for signaling only
Never skip connection cleanup logic for inactive or disconnected clients

faq.question: What is the difference between a hub and a group?
faq.answer: A hub is a top-level isolation boundary for connections. Groups are subsets of connections within a hub. Use hubs to separate different features and groups for targeted messaging within a feature.

faq.question: How do I authenticate clients connecting to Web PubSub?
faq.answer: Generate client access tokens server-side using the SDK. Clients use these tokens to connect. Tokens can include user IDs, group memberships, and role-based permissions.

faq.question: Can I send messages to users instead of connections?
faq.answer: Yes. Use sendToUser to reach all active connections for a specific user ID. This is useful when users have multiple devices or browser tabs open.

faq.question: What message formats does Web PubSub support?
faq.answer: The SDK supports TEXT_PLAIN for text messages and APPLICATION_JSON for structured data. Binary data can also be sent using BinaryData wrappers.

faq.question: How do I handle connection cleanup when users disconnect?
faq.answer: Use connection close events from your server webhook. Call closeConnection or closeUserConnections to clean up server-side state. Implement heartbeat logic to detect stale connections.

faq.question: Is there a message size limit?
faq.answer: Azure Web PubSub has service quotas for message size. Check current limits in Azure documentation. For large payloads, consider sending a notification and having clients fetch data separately.
